free_text: 15N ions deposited on beryllium foil. This foil had an
equivalent  thickness of 25 keV for 1 MeV protons,
CsI crystal used to detect alpha
particles and NaI for 4.43-MeV gamma radiation.
(p,alpha0) reactions were measured at nine angles.
For all measurements, the plane of the target was at
the angle of 45 degree with respect to the incident
beam. When the spectrometer was used to analyze the
scattered or reaction particles, the surface of the
target which contained the 15N faced the entrance to
the spectrometer. Therefore, for observations at
angles less than 90 degrees, the incident protons
passed through the beryllium foil before encountering
the surface containing the 15N. Corrections to the
energy scale have been made for this fact.
Scanned from Fig.2,4 in reference by IAEA-NDS.
(not per unit angle). Compiler assumes that the data
at 90 degrees are multiplied by 4 pi.
Digitized data were divided by 0.005 to express
